name: queryable-terminal-operations-extension
description: CollectionQueryable.toArray/first/count/any currently call backend.execute() which works for MemoryBackend. For SqlBackend + Postgres execution, need different flow: (1) Detect backend type or use separate PostgresBackend, (2) Call SqlBackend.compile*() to get [sql, params], (3) Execute via ISqlExecutor, (4) Hydrate results to MST via applySnapshot. Key challenge: field name normalization (snake_case DB columns to camelCase MST properties).
recommendation: Either: (A) Create PostgresBackend that wraps SqlBackend + BunSqlExecutor, or (B) Extend IBackend interface to support both patterns. Option A is cleaner separation. PostgresBackend.execute() does compile + execute + hydrate internally.
